## Manual Notes

## AI Runtime Project Design (PDGL) (v1)

<!-- PDGL:v1 START -->
### Project Identity
- Project Name: Workday Earnings Dashboard
- One-line Summary: A small front-end dashboard that shows todayâ€™s work progress and real-time earned income based on the userâ€™s schedule and pay settings.
- Target Users: Office workers, part-time workers, students, contractors, and anyone who wants a simple visual way to track workday progress and earnings.
- Non-goals: This is not a payroll system, tax calculator, HR tool, timesheet approval system, or financial advice product.

### Product / Runtime Intent
- What problem does this project solve?: It helps users understand how much of the workday has passed, how much time remains, and approximately how much money they have earned so far today.
- What should AI optimize for?: A calm, beautiful, accurate, and easy-to-use single-page dashboard with clear time and money calculations.
- What must AI avoid?: Do not overbuild payroll, tax, authentication, backend, team management, or complex enterprise features in the MVP.
- What is intentionally out of scope?: Tax deductions, payslip parsing, real payroll integration, employer compliance, overtime rules, superannuation, and multi-user accounts.

### Stack Decisions
- Language: TypeScript
- Framework: Next.js
- Runtime: Browser-first front-end application
- Package Manager: npm
- Database: None for MVP; use localStorage for saved settings
- Deployment Environment: Vercel or static-compatible Next.js deployment

### Runtime Constraints
- Files never touch: Do not manually edit generated `.next/`, `node_modules/`, lockfile unless dependency changes are required, or generated `.aidw/index/*`.
- Dangerous operations: Avoid destructive file commands, broad refactors, deleting configuration files, or changing package manager without confirmation.
- Deployment boundaries: MVP should remain deployable as a simple Next.js front-end app.
- Network restrictions: No external API is required for MVP.
- Command restrictions: Prefer `npm install`, `npm run dev`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build`.
- MCP write policy: Read-only by default. Any write operation should require explicit user confirmation.

### Development Workflow
- Preferred workflow: Define task â†’ update UI and calculation logic â†’ run lint/build â†’ summarize changes.
- Testing strategy: Start with manual UI verification and pure calculation helper tests if calculation logic is extracted.
- Definition of Done: User can enter work start/end time, break minutes, salary type, salary amount, and see live work progress plus earned amount.
- Required verification: Check progress calculation before work, during work, after work, and with invalid inputs.
- Snapshot expectations: UI should remain visually clean, light, glass-like, and dashboard-focused.

### Architecture Notes
- Entry points: `app/page.tsx` or main dashboard route.
- Directory conventions: Use `components/` for UI, `lib/` for calculation helpers, and `hooks/` for time/update logic if needed.
- Config sources: User settings should be stored in localStorage for MVP.
- Critical modules: Time progress calculation, salary normalization, earned amount calculation, settings form, circular progress display.
- Shared abstractions: Keep calculation functions pure and reusable.

### Bootstrap Guidance
- Recommended scaffold: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui.
- Manual setup steps: Create Next.js app, install Tailwind/shadcn, create dashboard layout, add localStorage persistence.
- Human-required setup: Choose project name, visual style, and default currency.
- Secrets/config setup expectations: No secrets required for MVP.

### AI Collaboration Rules
- How AI should propose changes: Keep changes small, explain calculation assumptions, and avoid adding backend features unless requested.
- How AI should ask for clarification: Ask only when salary rules, break handling, or workday assumptions are unclear.
- Preferred output structure: Summary of change, files modified, calculation assumptions, verification steps, next actions.
- What requires confirmation: Adding backend, authentication, database, external APIs, payroll/tax logic, or changing the visual design direction.
<!-- PDGL:v1 END -->

## Stable Human Context (SHC) (v1)

<!-- SHC:v1 START -->
### Project Goal
- Build a polished single-page dashboard that shows todayâ€™s work progress and real-time estimated earnings.

### Target Users
- Workers who want a simple and motivating way to see how much of the workday is complete and how much they have earned so far.

### Non-goals
- Do not build payroll, tax advice, employer management, payslip import, or complex HR features in the MVP.

### Stack Decisions
- Next.js
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- shadcn/ui
- localStorage for user preferences
- No backend for MVP

### Runtime Constraints
- Browser-first app.
- No external API required.
- Calculations are estimates only.
- Keep salary logic simple and transparent.

### Directory Conventions
- `app/` for pages and layout.
- `components/` for dashboard UI.
- `components/ui/` for shadcn components.
- `lib/` for salary and time calculation helpers.
- `hooks/` for live clock/update logic if needed.

### Config Sources
- User schedule and salary settings are stored in localStorage.
- Default currency should be AUD.
- Default work week can be 5 days.
- Default break time can be 0 or 30 minutes depending on the UI choice.

### Testing Strategy
- Verify calculation behavior before work starts, during work, after work ends, and when break time is applied.
- Verify annual, monthly, fortnightly, and hourly salary modes.
- Verify invalid or missing inputs do not crash the UI.

### Release Constraints
- MVP should build successfully with `npm run build`.
- UI should be responsive on desktop and usable on mobile.
- Do not introduce unnecessary dependencies.

### Files Never Touch
- `node_modules/`
- `.next/`
- generated `.aidw/index/*`
- generated `.aidw/context/tasks.json`
- generated `.aidw/scan/last.json`

### Deployment Boundaries
- Deploy as a simple Next.js front-end app.
- Prefer Vercel for the first release.
- No server-side secrets or database required.
<!-- SHC:v1 END -->

## Project Role

Use `PROJECT.md` as the human-owned project context and `.aidw/AI_project.md` as the generated AI-readable project context. Keep stable architecture notes, workflow constraints, and editing boundaries here so AI tools can understand the repository before suggesting or implementing changes.

## AI Working Rules

### Before Starting Any Task

AI must read:

- `AGENTS.md`\r\n- `PROJECT.md`\r\n- `.aidw/AI_project.md`
- `.aidw/rules.md` if it exists
- Relevant task file under `task/` if working on a task

Do not proceed without understanding this context. If context appears outdated, recommend running `rck scan`.

### Before Starting Implementation

If expected behavior, scope boundaries, affected components, or acceptance criteria are unclear, pause and ask focused clarification questions. Do not start coding on vague requests.

### Before Marking Task As Done

AI must verify:

- The change matches the task requirements.
- No unrelated files were modified.
- Existing patterns and structure are preserved.
- Tests, if any, are updated or still pass.
- No generated files were manually edited.

## Editing Boundaries

- Reuse existing components, modules, utilities, and services before creating new structures.
- Keep changes minimal and localized.
- Preserve backward compatibility for shared or widely used code.
- Do not hand-edit generated `.aidw/index/*`, `.aidw/context/tasks.json`, or `.aidw/scan/last.json`.
- Update relevant documentation when user-facing behavior changes.

## Task System

- Create tasks with `rck task new "Task title"`.
- `task/task.md` is the human-readable task registry.
- `task/T-xxx-*.md` files contain detailed implementation tasks.
- `.aidw/context/tasks.json` is generated by `scan`; do not edit it directly.
- Keep task IDs stable and registry rows in sync with task files.

## Workflow

1. Run `rck init`.
2. Run `rck scan`.
3. Review `PROJECT.md` and `.aidw/AI_project.md`.
4. Add stable manual notes.
5. Use AI coding tools with this context.
6. Re-run `scan` after structural, task, or metadata changes.
